metadata.description: Synthetic test-failure corpus for RAG triage eval. 50 records, 10 per category. Fully synthetic — no real Salesforce or production data.
metadata.note: Records are intentionally built in similarity clusters (pairs sharing a root-cause pattern) to give retrieval eval real structure. See cluster_hint field.

records.stack_trace:
AssertionError: expected order status 'CONFIRMED' but was 'PENDING'
  at test_checkout.py:142 in test_order_confirmed_after_payment
  at OrderAssertions.assert_status (assertions.py:88)
records.log_snippet:
12:04:11.201 INFO  payment webhook dispatched
12:04:11.203 INFO  assertion evaluated status=PENDING
12:04:11.640 INFO  webhook processed status=CONFIRMED (arrived 437ms after assertion)
records.true_root_cause: Race condition: test asserts order status before the async payment webhook has been processed. Passes when the webhook is fast, fails when it lags.

records.stack_trace:
AssertionError: expected notification count 1 but was 0
  at test_notify.py:77 in test_email_sent_on_signup
  at NotifyAssertions.assert_count (assertions.py:88)
records.log_snippet:
09:31:02.010 INFO  signup event emitted
09:31:02.011 INFO  assertion checked mailbox size=0
09:31:02.250 INFO  async consumer delivered email 239ms later
records.true_root_cause: Race condition: assertion runs before the asynchronous email consumer processes the queued event. Nondeterministic based on consumer latency.

records.stack_trace:
TimeoutError: waited 5000ms for selector '#dashboard-loaded'
  at test_login_ui.py:53 in test_dashboard_renders
  at WebDriver.wait_for (driver.py:210)
records.log_snippet:
14:22:40.500 INFO  navigated to /dashboard
14:22:45.501 ERROR selector '#dashboard-loaded' not found within 5000ms
14:22:46.900 INFO  element appeared 1399ms after timeout (slow CI runner)
records.true_root_cause: UI wait timeout too tight for a slow CI runner; the element renders but past the 5s window. Environment-speed-dependent, not a real defect.

records.stack_trace:
TimeoutError: waited 3000ms for selector '.toast-success'
  at test_profile_ui.py:118 in test_save_profile_shows_toast
  at WebDriver.wait_for (driver.py:210)
records.log_snippet:
16:08:12.100 INFO  clicked save
16:08:15.101 ERROR toast '.toast-success' not visible in 3000ms
16:08:15.480 INFO  toast rendered 379ms late under CI load
records.true_root_cause: Explicit-wait window too short under CI load; the success toast appears but after the assertion window. Timing-dependent flake.

records.stack_trace:
AssertionError: expected cache empty but found 3 entries
  at test_cache.py:64 in test_cache_starts_empty
  at CacheAssertions.assert_empty (assertions.py:120)
records.log_snippet:
11:00:01.000 INFO  test_cache_starts_empty began
11:00:01.002 WARN  cache contained residue from prior test test_cache_populated
11:00:01.003 ERROR shared cache not reset between tests
records.true_root_cause: Order-dependent test: shared cache state leaks from a previous test because there is no teardown. Passes in isolation, fails when run after another test.

records.stack_trace:
AssertionError: expected user count 0 but was 2
  at test_users.py:39 in test_no_users_initially
  at DbAssertions.assert_row_count (assertions.py:145)
records.log_snippet:
10:15:00.010 INFO  test_no_users_initially began
10:15:00.011 WARN  users table held rows created by test_create_user
10:15:00.012 ERROR test isolation broken: no transaction rollback
records.true_root_cause: Test isolation failure: previous test's DB writes were not rolled back, so row counts leak between tests. Order-dependent flake.

records.stack_trace:
AssertionError: expected shuffled list != original but they were equal
  at test_shuffle.py:28 in test_shuffle_changes_order
  at ListAssertions.assert_not_equal (assertions.py:60)
records.log_snippet:
08:45:33.100 INFO  random seed not fixed for test run
08:45:33.101 WARN  shuffle produced identity permutation (1/n! chance)
08:45:33.102 ERROR nondeterministic assertion failed this run
records.true_root_cause: Unseeded randomness: shuffle occasionally returns the original order, failing the assertion. Nondeterministic because the RNG seed is not pinned in the test.

records.stack_trace:
AssertionError: expected token expiry in future but was in past
  at test_token.py:91 in test_token_not_expired
  at TimeAssertions.assert_future (assertions.py:200)
records.log_snippet:
23:59:58.900 INFO  token issued ttl=2s
23:59:59.100 INFO  assertion ran near midnight boundary
00:00:01.050 ERROR token expired before assertion due to test wall-clock drift
records.true_root_cause: Time-dependent test: a short TTL combined with real wall-clock timing near a boundary makes the token expire mid-test. Fails intermittently based on execution speed and clock.

records.stack_trace:
requests.exceptions.ConnectionError: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='api.stripe-sandbox.test'): Max retries exceeded
  at test_payment_gateway.py:47 in test_charge_succeeds
  at PaymentClient.charge (client.py:88)
records.log_snippet:
13:20:10.000 INFO  calling sandbox payment gateway
13:20:12.500 WARN  sandbox returned 503 (intermittent)
13:20:12.501 INFO  retry on rerun succeeded — third-party sandbox flaky
records.true_root_cause: Intermittent third-party sandbox outage (503) makes the test fail nondeterministically. Not our defect; the external dependency is unreliable.

records.stack_trace:
socket.timeout: The read operation timed out

records.log_snippet:
17:44:01.000 INFO  querying external geocoding API
17:44:11.001 WARN  no response in 10s (external API latency spike)
17:44:30.000 INFO  same request succeeded on rerun
records.true_root_cause: External geocoding API latency spike causes an intermittent read timeout. Rerun passes; the flake originates in an unreliable third-party dependency.

records.stack_trace:
psycopg2.OperationalError: could not connect to server: Connection timed out
  at db/pool.py:132 in acquire_connection
  at test_orders_integration.py:22 in setup

02:11:03.000 ERROR Postgres pool exhausted: 20/20 connections in use

records.true_root_cause: Postgres connection pool exhaustion in the test environment: all 20 connections held, new acquisitions time out. Environment capacity issue, not application logic.

records.stack_trace:

  at test_reporting.py:41 in setup
records.log_snippet:
03:50:09.000 ERROR Postgres max_connections=100 reached
03:50:09.010 ERROR new connection rejected: slots reserved for superuser
03:50:09.011 INFO  parallel test shards over-subscribed the DB
records.true_root_cause: Database max_connections ceiling hit because parallel test shards opened too many connections against a shared test DB. Environment resource limit.

records.stack_trace:
MemoryError
  at etl/transform.py:210 in load_batch
  at test_etl_pipeline.py:88 in test_large_batch
Killed (OOMKilled)
records.log_snippet:
04:30:00.000 INFO  worker pod memory limit 512Mi
04:30:41.000 WARN  RSS climbed to 508Mi loading batch
04:30:41.500 ERROR container OOMKilled, exit code 137
records.true_root_cause: Worker pod OOMKilled: the 512Mi memory limit is too small for the batch size, so the kernel kills the container (exit 137). Environment sizing issue.

records.stack_trace:
java.lang.OutOfMemoryError: Java heap space
  at ImageResizer.resize(ImageResizer.java:144)
  at test_thumbnail_gen.py:60 in test_batch_resize
Killed (OOMKilled)
records.log_snippet:
05:12:00.000 INFO  JVM -Xmx256m in test pod
05:12:18.000 WARN  heap usage 250m during batch resize
05:12:18.900 ERROR OutOfMemoryError, pod OOMKilled exit 137
records.true_root_cause: JVM heap (-Xmx256m) too small for the image batch, causing OutOfMemoryError and pod OOMKill. Environment/resource-limit problem, not a code defect.

records.stack_trace:

07:00:00.000 ERROR DNS lookup failed for inventory-db.internal

records.true_root_cause: Cluster DNS (CoreDNS) was mid-restart, so the internal hostname failed to resolve. Transient environment/network problem, not application code.

records.stack_trace:
OSError: [Errno 28] No space left on device

  at test_export.py:102 in test_write_report

01:20:00.000 ERROR /tmp at 100% (10Gi) on CI node
01:20:00.010 WARN  accumulated artifacts from prior runs not cleaned
01:20:00.011 ERROR write failed: No space left on device
records.true_root_cause: CI node disk (/tmp) filled to 100% by uncleaned artifacts from prior runs, so the write failed with ENOSPC. Environment/housekeeping issue.

records.stack_trace:

  at parsers/profile.py:41 in parse

11:30:00.000 INFO  profile API refactor merged in commit e4f5g6h
11:30:00.010 ERROR field 'userId' now nested under 'user.id'
11:30:00.011 ERROR flat-access parser broke
records.true_root_cause: A refactor nested the previously top-level userId under user.id, breaking consumers that read the flat field. Schema-change regression from a code commit.

09:10:00.010 ERROR caller assumed non-null user, dereferenced .email
09:10:00.011 ERROR NoneType attribute access
records.true_root_cause: A commit changed get_user() to return None on a cache miss; a downstream caller still assumed a non-null object and dereferenced .email. Null-handling regression.
